title: The Daily Schedule of a Six-Figure Rental Agent
dek: Volume leasing looks chaotic from outside. Inside, the strong months come from a repeating daily structure. Here's the honest shape of the day, hour by hour.
heroAlt: The Daily Schedule of a Six-Figure Rental Agent

blocks.html: Overnight inquiries answered first (relocating clients email at strange hours; speed wins them). Today's showings confirmed. Availability updates from the Power 25 buildings checked and logged in the Building Bible. Two or three relationship touches sent before most agents have opened a laptop.

Tours are clustered geographically, which is why the Power 25 favors buildings you can walk between. Gaps between showings are building time: a hello to a leasing office, notes updated, the walk-in introduction at a target property.
Real lunch, and the day's content: one post, engagement replies, fifteen minutes, no more. Sustainable beats heroic.

blocks.text: Consistency looks boring right up until it looks inevitable.

blocks.html: The second showing block, then application chasing: documents reviewed, leasing offices nudged, clients updated. This unglamorous ninety minutes is where already-won deals are protected.

Follow-ups from today's tours (AI drafts, human edits), the Bible updated, tomorrow's route planned, and the touch rhythm advanced. Evening showings happen some days; the structure flexes, the blocks don't disappear.
Monday pipeline-heavy, midweek showing-heavy, Friday admin and relationship maintenance, and a genuine day of rest, because this is a rhythm business and rhythms need recovery.

That's the whole secret: no magic, just blocks, repeated. Month 1 of this series ends here. If you want the weekly system that keeps it going, The Lease Up lands every Saturday, free, link below. Better agents win.
